content.text: Claude is available through Anthropic's direct API and through a growing set of platform partnerships that embed Claude into cloud infrastructure, enterprise software, and developer tools. This sub-subtopic maps the major partner categories, explains what each integration adds over the direct API, and gives concrete code for the most common integrations.

The two most significant cloud partnerships are Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud Vertex AI. Both host Claude within their respective cloud infrastructure, enabling teams to access the model without routing traffic to Anthropic's servers directly. The benefits are operational rather than functional — the model behavior is identical to the direct API.
GCP-native billing, CMEK, regional data residency
Azure Private Link, Azure Monitor, compliance
Bedrock integrates with IAM for authentication, enabling fine-grained permission policies and role-based access without managing API keys:

import boto3
import json

# Using IAM role (recommended for EC2/Lambda/ECS)
# No explicit credentials needed — boto3 picks up the instance role
bedrock = boto3.client("bedrock-runtime", region_name="us-east-1")

response = bedrock.invoke_model(
    modelId="anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0",
    body=json.dumps({
        "anthropic_version": "bedrock-2023-05-31",
        "max_tokens": 1024,
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello from Bedrock"}]
    }),
    contentType="application/json",
    accept="application/json"
)

result = json.loads(response["body"].read())
print(result["content"][0]["text"])
For teams that prefer the Anthropic SDK interface over boto3, the
, which wraps boto3 and exposes the same

import anthropic

# AnthropicBedrock uses boto3 credentials under the hood
client = anthropic.AnthropicBedrock(
    aws_region="us-east-1",
    # Optional: explicit credentials for local dev
    # aws_access_key="...",
    # aws_secret_key="...",
)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)
print(message.content[0].text)

Use the AnthropicBedrock client in all Python code — it lets you swap back to the direct API by changing one line (the client constructor), making it easy to test against Anthropic's API when debugging Bedrock-specific issues.

# Enable the Vertex AI API and authenticate
gcloud auth application-default login
pip install anthropic[vertex]

import anthropic

client = anthropic.AnthropicVertex(
    project_id="my-gcp-project",
    region="us-east5",
)

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-haiku@20251001",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello from Vertex"}]
)
print(response.content[0].text)
Vertex AI enforces regional data residency — pick the region where your data must stay. As of mid-2025, Claude is available in
, and a growing set of GCP regions. Anthropic publishes the current region list in the Vertex AI documentation.
Observability tools wrap the Anthropic SDK to capture every request, response, latency, cost, and token count without changing your application logic. Three widely-used options:
Helicone intercepts API calls by routing through its proxy. Zero code changes required — only a header and a base URL swap:
Langfuse provides tracing, cost tracking, and prompt management. It uses an SDK decorator pattern rather than a proxy:
LangSmith integrates automatically when you use LangChain. Set environment variables and every chain/agent run is traced:

export LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true
export LANGCHAIN_API_KEY=ls__...
export LANGCHAIN_PROJECT=my-claude-project

from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropic
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

# With env vars set above, all calls are automatically traced in LangSmith
llm = ChatAnthropic(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")
chain = ChatPromptTemplate.from_template("Summarize: {text}") | llm
result = chain.invoke({"text": "Long document text here..."})
# → appears in LangSmith with input/output/latency/tokens

Zapier, Make (formerly Integromat), and n8n allow teams to connect Claude to hundreds of SaaS tools without writing code. These are appropriate for internal tooling, content workflows, and prototypes — not for customer-facing production systems where you need full control over prompts, error handling, and cost.
No streaming; no tool use; 5s timeout on some plans
Rate limits managed by Make, not directly controllable
Requires self-hosting for data residency guarantees

Major enterprise platforms embed Claude as an AI layer inside their own products. For developers, this means Claude is available through the platform's native API — you do not call the Anthropic API directly.
Apex classes call Einstein API; no direct Anthropic calls
Flow Designer actions; scripted REST from ServiceNow
Document processing, code generation in SAP IDE
SAP AI Core SDK with Anthropic model ID

Enterprise platform integrations are consumed by end users and Salesforce/ServiceNow/SAP developers, not by API developers. If you are building a custom integration, use the direct Anthropic API or a cloud provider SDK rather than routing through an enterprise platform's abstraction layer.

Several vector database providers offer native Anthropic integrations that simplify the RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline. The integration handles embedding generation and vector search; you provide the Claude call for generation:

import anthropic
from pinecone import Pinecone

# Pinecone for retrieval + Anthropic for generation
pc = Pinecone(api_key=PINECONE_API_KEY)
index = pc.Index("my-docs")
client = anthropic.Anthropic()

def rag_query(question: str, top_k: int = 3) -> str:
    # 1. Embed the question (using any embedding model)
    import voyageai
    vo = voyageai.Client(api_key=VOYAGE_API_KEY)
    query_embedding = vo.embed([question], model="voyage-3").embeddings[0]

    # 2. Retrieve relevant chunks from Pinecone
    results = index.query(vector=query_embedding, top_k=top_k, include_metadata=True)
    context = "\n\n".join(r["metadata"]["text"] for r in results["matches"])

    # 3. Generate answer with Claude
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=1024,
        system="Answer questions using only the provided context. If the context doesn't contain the answer, say so.",
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": f"Context:\n{context}\n\nQuestion: {question}"
        }]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

answer = rag_query("What is the refund policy for enterprise plans?")
print(answer)
Voyage AI is Anthropic's recommended embedding provider — it was specifically optimized for use with Claude. Use
Before adopting any partner integration, answer these five questions:
Does it use the official Anthropic SDK under the hood?
If yes, you get full feature support and can verify behavior against the direct API. If it uses an older HTTP implementation, it may lag on new model features.
Does your data pass through the partner's servers (proxy model) or go directly to Anthropic? Proxy models add latency and a third-party data processor.
How does it handle errors and rate limits?
Partners should surface Anthropic's 429 errors and propagate stop_reason correctly. Test this before committing.
When Anthropic releases a new model, how long until the partner integration supports it? For cloud providers, the lag is typically 2-4 weeks.

If you switch from Bedrock to the direct API tomorrow, how much code changes? Low lock-in means the SDK interface is identical (AnthropicBedrock) vs high lock-in means you rewrote your call structure around the partner's API.

When your application may need to run against multiple Claude access points — direct API in development, Bedrock in production, Vertex in a GCP-deployed instance — an abstraction layer that swaps backends via configuration prevents rewriting call sites:
The Bedrock and Vertex clients accept the same
parameters as the direct client — the only difference is the model ID format and the authentication mechanism. This makes environment-based switching reliable.

Store the backend selection in an environment variable or a configuration file, not in code. This lets you promote the same Docker image from development (direct API) to staging (Bedrock) to production (Bedrock with a different IAM role) without rebuilding.
Model pricing is set by Anthropic and is the same across access methods (direct API, Bedrock, Vertex) on a per-token basis. The differences are in additional charges at the platform layer:
AWS data transfer rates if cross-region

For high-volume production workloads, the platform surcharges on Bedrock and Vertex are small relative to token costs. The dominant cost driver is always token consumption, not the access layer. The Batch API (direct or via Bedrock) provides a 50% discount on token costs for asynchronous workloads regardless of access method.
Every partner integration adds a layer to the trust chain. Audit each layer before deploying to production:

Your prompts and completions pass through a third-party server. Verify SOC 2 Type II compliance and ensure DPA (Data Processing Agreement) terms cover your data classification. For PHI or financial data, prefer the decorator pattern (Langfuse) over proxy-based tools.
Data stays within the cloud provider's network. Encryption at rest and in transit is standard. Check whether the provider's compliance certifications (HIPAA BAA, FedRAMP) match your requirements.
Treat these as low-trust for sensitive data. Build with synthetic or anonymized data; add a PII-scrubbing step before prompts leave your control.
The platform vendor controls data access. Audit their Anthropic data processing commitments in the platform's trust documentation, not just their general privacy policy.

# PII scrubbing before sending to any third-party integration
import re

PII_PATTERNS = [
    (r"\b[A-Z][a-z]+ [A-Z][a-z]+\b", "[NAME]"),          # full names (heuristic)
    (r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b", "[SSN]"),                # SSN
    (r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b", "[EMAIL]"),
    (r"\b\+?1?[-.\s]?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b", "[PHONE]"),
]

def scrub_pii(text: str) -> str:
    for pattern, replacement in PII_PATTERNS:
        text = re.sub(pattern, replacement, text)
    return text

# Apply before sending to Zapier or any proxy-based integration
safe_prompt = scrub_pii(user_input)

The Anthropic partner ecosystem spans cloud providers (Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Azure AI Foundry), observability tools (Helicone, Langfuse, LangSmith), no-code automation (Zapier, Make, n8n), enterprise platform embeddings (Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP), and vector database integrations (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector). Cloud provider integrations use IAM-based auth and enable data residency and VPC routing. Observability tools add tracing and cost tracking via proxy or decorator patterns with minimal code changes. When evaluating a partner integration, assess data flow, feature lag, and lock-in cost — the AnthropicBedrock and AnthropicVertex clients score well on all three by using the same SDK interface as the direct API.
